description: Back to jobs BIM Manager Memphis, TN; Remote Apply About xAI xAI’s mission is to create AI systems that can accurately understand the universe and aid humanity in its pursuit of knowledge. Our team is small, highly motivated, and focused on engineering excellence. This organization is for individuals who appreciate challenging themselves and thrive on curiosity. We operate with a flat organizational structure. All employees are expected to be hands-on and to contribute directly to the company’s mission. Leadership is given to those who show initiative and consistently deliver excellence. Work ethic and strong prioritization skills are important. All employees are expected to have strong communication skills. They should be able to concisely and accurately share knowledge with their teammates. ABOUT THE ROLE: We are seeking an experienced BIM Manager to join our engineering team. In this role, you will serve as the primary advocate for Building Information Modeling (BIM) and document control processes on behalf of the owner across multiple capital projects. You will ensure high-quality, consistent, and coordinated digital designs from design through construction, while also providing hands-on Revit drafting support for electrical and mechanical systems. This position combines strategic BIM and document control leadership with practical modeling/drafting in MEP disciplines to protect owner interests, verify design quality, identify risks early, and facilitate better decision-making throughout the project lifecycle. RESPONSIBILITIES: Develop, implement, and enforce BIM standards, protocols, and execution plans Lead interdisciplinary model coordination reviews to identify and resolve clashes, inconsistencies, and potential issues before they impact construction Act as the owner's point of contact for all BIM-related matters, collaborating with design consultants, contractors, and stakeholders to align on BIM goals, data exchange formats, LOD/LOI requirements, and model federation Perform hands-on drafting and modeling in Autodesk Revit, including updating MEP systems (power distribution, piping, ductwork, etc.) Maintain and manage central owner-side BIM repositories, ensuring version control, data integrity, and secure access for internal teams and project partners. Conduct quality assurance checks on models, including geometric accuracy and data richness Support project handover by ensuring as-built models are complete, accurate, and suitable for facility management/operations Provide guidance to engineers on BIM tools, workflows, and best practices Stay current with evolving BIM technologies, standards, and industry trends, recommending improvements to enhance project outcomes for the owner Implement and maintain project-specific document control procedures and workflows Receive, review, register, distribute, and track incoming and outgoing documents, ensuring proper metadata, numbering, revision control, and approval workflows BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: 7+ years of experience in BIM management and Revit modeling, with at least 3–5 years focused on MEP (mechanical and electrical) systems Advanced proficiency in Autodesk Revit (MEP focus), BIM 360/ACC, AutoCAD, and related tools (Navisworks a plus) Strong understanding of BIM processes across the project lifecycle, including model coordination, clash detection, LOD specifications, and data exchange standards Strong understanding of document lifecycle management, version/revision control, and transmittal processes Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with tight deadlines and changing priorities PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE: Experience working on the owner's side, as an owner's representative, or in a client-facing role reviewing consultant deliverables Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving abilities Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to bridge technical BIM details and owner/business objectives xAI is an equal opportunity employer. Our team is small, highly motivated, and focused on engineering excellence. This organization is for individuals who appreciate challenging themselves and thrive on curiosity. We operate with a flat organizational structure. All employees are expected to be hands-on and to contribute directly to the company’s mission. Leadership is given to those who show initiative and consistently deliver excellence. Work ethic and strong prioritization skills are important. All employees are expected to have strong communication skills. They should be able to concisely and accurately share knowledge with their teammates. ABOUT THE ROLE: We are seeking an exceptional Construction Manager to lead the construction of a state-of-the-art Doosan combined cycle power plant that will deliver reliable, high-capacity power to xAI's supercomputing infrastructure in the Memphis region. This high-visibility, mission-critical role will oversee the full lifecycle of a large-scale natural gas-fired combined cycle facility, incorporating Doosan Enerbility's advanced gas turbines, steam turbines, HRSGs, balance-of-plant systems, and supporting infrastructure. You will drive aggressive timelines, ensure world-class safety and quality, manage complex stakeholder interfaces, and deliver the power foundation required to scale AI training at unprecedented levels. This is a full-time, onsite role based in Texas, with potential travel to vendor sites. RESPONSIBILITIES: Lead end-to-end construction of the Doosan combined cycle power plant, from site preparation and civil works through mechanical/electrical installation, commissioning, and handover. Serve as xAI's primary on-site leader, managing interfaces with Doosan Enerbility, EPC contractors, subcontractors, utilities, and regulatory authorities. Develop and maintain detailed construction schedules, resource plans, and progress tracking (using Primavera P6 or similar tools) to meet xAI's aggressive delivery targets. Oversee procurement, logistics, erection, and integration of major equipment including gas turbines, steam turbines, generators, HRSGs, cooling towers, fuel systems, and electrical substations. Enforce rigorous safety, environmental, and quality standards, ensuring full compliance with permits, emissions controls, OSHA, EPA, and local regulations. Manage project budget, cost forecasting, change orders, and risk mitigation; proactively resolve issues to avoid delays or cost overruns. Coordinate commissioning, startup, performance testing, and seamless transition to operations teams. Lead and motivate large field teams (200+ personnel), subcontractors, and vendors in a high-velocity environment. Provide regular progress updates, risk assessments, and key metrics to xAI senior leadership. BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: 15+ years of progressive construction management experience on large-scale industrial projects, with 8+ years specifically leading combined cycle power plant construction (500 MW+ scale preferred). Proven track record delivering gas-fired combined cycle facilities on time and budget, including turbine erection, HRSG installation, and balance-of-plant integration. Deep expertise in power plant construction sequencing, major equipment installation, piping, electrical systems, and grid interconnection. Strong knowledge of U.S. permitting processes, environmental compliance (air quality, emissions, noise), and utility coordination. Demonstrated leadership managing large teams, multiple subcontractors, and international OEM vendors under pressure. Proficiency with project management tools (Primavera P6, MS Project), cost control, and risk management. Ability to thrive in fast-moving, high-ambiguity environments with a no-excuses mindset. PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE: Direct experience with Doosan Enerbility (or similar OEMs like GE, Siemens, Mitsubishi) combined cycle projects. Background in power infrastructure supporting data centers, hyperscalers, or mission-critical facilities. Familiarity with fast-track construction approaches in the tech/energy sector. Bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, or related field; PE license or advanced degree a plus. Knowledge of advanced emissions controls and sustainability practices in power generation. ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS: Willingness to work extended hours and be fully onsite during peak construction phases. Ability to stand/walk for long periods, work in industrial environments, and travel as needed. xAI is an equal opportunity employer. Back to jobs New Construction Supervisor (Night Shift), Power Generation Plant Memphis, TN Apply About xAI xAI’s mission is to create AI systems that can accurately understand the universe and aid humanity in its pursuit of knowledge. Our team is small, highly motivated, and focused on engineering excellence. This organization is for individuals who appreciate challenging themselves and thrive on curiosity. We operate with a flat organizational structure. All employees are expected to be hands-on and to contribute directly to the company’s mission. Leadership is given to those who show initiative and consistently deliver excellence. Work ethic and strong prioritization skills are important. All employees are expected to have strong communication skills. They should be able to concisely and accurately share knowledge with their teammates. ABOUT THE ROLE: You will oversee teams and contractors building critical power generation infrastructure — including concrete foundations, structural pours, underground electrical conduits/piping, turbine and generator supports, process equipment foundations, cooling systems, and related utilities — in a fast-moving, mission-critical environment. If you thrive on hands-on leadership during night shifts, complex industrial construction, and contributing to the power backbone for advanced AI infrastructure, join us to help build one of the most impactful power projects supporting the world's largest AI training systems. RESPONSIBILITIES: Oversee and coordinate concrete pouring, forming, finishing, and underground utility installations (electrical conduits, piping, manifolds, grounding systems, and process infrastructure) to meet the exacting specifications of a large-scale power generation facility, including gas turbine or combined-cycle plant components. Manage and schedule contractors, subcontractors, and on-site night shift crews to deliver project milestones for xAI’s power plant on time and to xAI’s stringent quality and safety standards. Ensure all contractors adhere to scope, delivering high-integrity concrete structures, underground electrical and utility systems, and equipment foundations critical to power generation, transmission support, and auxiliary systems. Monitor daily (night) progress of power plant construction, maintaining detailed logs, plot plans, and as-built documentation for foundations, piping/conduit networks, structural supports, and related infrastructure. Develop and enforce aggressive construction schedules while helping keep the multi-million-dollar power project on time and within budget in a high-velocity environment. Champion and enforce xAI’s safety program, building a zero-incident culture across employees, contractors, and subcontractors working around heavy process equipment, excavations, high-voltage areas, and industrial power systems during night operations. Lead, train, and supervise foremen and crews on the night shift, fostering teamwork, accountability, and effective communication in a high-stakes industrial power project. Proactively resolve on-site challenges — material delays, technical conflicts in underground electrical/process systems, sequencing issues, or night-shift coordination — with a focus on power plant reliability and uptime. Conduct regular site inspections to verify compliance with local regulations, environmental permits, safety protocols (especially for night work and heavy equipment), and xAI’s quality standards for power infrastructure. Prepare and present clear progress reports, construction metrics, and risk updates to senior leadership, highlighting night-shift accomplishments and any handover items. BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: 5+ years managing construction teams with strong emphasis on concrete work and underground utilities for large-scale industrial projects. 5+ years direct hands-on experience in concrete pouring, forming, finishing, and underground infrastructure (electrical conduits, piping, foundations) — ideally on power plants, substations, gas turbine installations, combined-cycle facilities, or other heavy industrial/power projects. High school diploma or equivalency certificate. Availability and willingness to work full night shift (with potential flexibility for evenings/weekends as site needs dictate). PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE: Postsecondary diploma or degree in construction management, civil engineering, electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, or related field. Proven track record supervising concrete and underground construction for power generation plants, substations, turbine foundations, cooling systems, or similar process-industrial/power projects. Familiarity with Memphis/Southaven-area building codes, environmental regulations, and industrial permitting (TDEC, MDEQ, electrical/utility rules, or similar). Experience with construction management software (Procore, AutoCAD, MS Project, Primavera, etc.) in complex infrastructure or power builds. Deep knowledge of construction critical paths, sequencing, and workflows for power generation/process facilities through commissioning and startup. Strong understanding of concrete specifications, grounding, waterproofing, and underground systems designed for electrical conveyance, heavy equipment foundations, and harsh industrial/power environments. Demonstrated ability to lead diverse teams under tight deadlines in industrial/power projects while maintaining collaboration, safety, and night-shift productivity. Expert-level commitment to safety and environmental best practices, with history of establishing strong safety cultures on regulated industrial sites (including night operations, lighting, and fatigue management). Excellent problem-solving and conflict-resolution skills for technically complex power/infrastructure scenarios. Proficiency with Microsoft Office suite (Excel, PowerPoint, Word, Teams, Project) for reporting and scheduling. ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS: Subject to post-employment random drug testing. Subject to pre-employment and annual background checks. Comfortable working outdoors/in industrial environments at night with noise, heavy equipment, excavations, process piping, limited lighting, and power systems. Flexible availability for evenings/weekends as site needs dictate (night shift primary). Ability to lift 25+ lbs and perform physically demanding tasks in outdoor/tight conditions during night hours. xAI is an equal opportunity employer. ABOUT THE ROLE: As a Datacenter Operations Technician at xAI, you will be responsible for the health of our server and network infrastructure for Data Centers and Global Points of Presence. You will be responsible for our two most important data center operations metrics: mean time to detect (MTTD) and mean time to repair (MTTR). You are great at following directions, are a fast learner and are super attentive to details. You are accessible via cell phone and willing to get onsite when there are emergencies as needed. You understand all aspects of the equipment you are responsible for to drive efficiencies and optimizations where necessary to simplify and improve our server intake and repair processes. RESPONSIBILITIES: Report to the job site during initial construction and report back to the engineering team as required. Performing troubleshooting and monitoring of the servers and network in our data centers and global points of presence Rack and stack of data center network equipment Maintain Warehouse inventory and asset management using our internal application. Labeling and troubleshooting for fiber/optics cables Power supply cabling, installation, troubleshooting and repair Installation of racks, servers and switches; this includes staging racks in place, cabling, power up and handoff of hardware to the provisioning team for customer capacity allocation. Manage, response and resolution of data center operations tickets used cross functionally within xAI via Jira. Create and Maintain documentation of tasks and standard operating procedures Receipt and Decommissioning of data center hardware. Vendor returns for infrastructure under and out of warranty Manage spare parts inventory within the data center Define, design, and implement network layouts and solutions within our data centers BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: High school diploma or equivalency certificate 2+ years of experience working with server, storage, compute and network hardware 2+ Years of experience troubleshooting and repairing servers and networking infrastructure 2+ years of experience in Inventory Management, and ordering, receiving and shipping server and network equipment Position is subject to pre-employment drug and random drug and alcohol testing Position is subject to pre-employment and annual post employment background checks Physical Requirements: Work is often performed in tight quarters and physical dexterity is necessary to perform job functions Ability to lift up to 35lbs. unassisted Comfortable working at elevated heights (up to 50 feet) with appropriate safety gear Comfortable working in an environment requiring exposure to noise Available to work evenings and weekends, as the schedule varies depending on site operational needs; flexibility is required PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE: Strong Linux skills including: Navigating the system’s directories and filing system Manipulating files in the Linux shell User permission configuration Package installation and software management Ability to identify and apply different filesystem types Using Linux commands for process management Basic troubleshooting and debugging Bash or other scripting Experience being on-call and ability to respond to critical events as needed. Experience leading Data Center Infrastructure projects. Curious to always learn new things within the Data Center World Excellent prioritization and time management skills Able to work in a fast paced environment Detail Oriented Oracle Experience Inventory Management 4+ years of experience in Structured Cabling Copper/Fiber 4+ years of experience in Power and Cooling concepts inside the data center xAI is an equal opportunity employer. ABOUT THE ROLE: We are seeking an experienced Electrical Commissioning Engineer to join our infrastructure team. In this role, you will lead and execute the commissioning, testing, and handover of critical electrical systems supporting xAI's AI supercomputing facilities. Your scope includes power generation, electrical distribution, BESS, UPS, generators, switchgear, and related infrastructure. You will ensure systems are fully operational, reliable, and compliant with design intent, safety standards, and performance requirements before transitioning to operations. This hands-on position combines strategic commissioning planning with on-site execution to minimize risks, validate performance, and enable rapid scaling of high-density compute environments. RESPONSIBILITIES: Develop, implement, and maintain comprehensive commissioning plans, protocols, test procedures, checklists, and scripts for electrical systems with related control systems Lead functional, integrated systems, and performance testing (including factory acceptance testing (FAT), site acceptance testing (SAT), and load bank testing) to verify system readiness and compliance with specifications Coordinate with design engineers, construction teams, vendors, and operations to resolve issues, perform troubleshooting, and execute corrective actions during commissioning phases Conduct thorough documentation of test results, deficiencies, punch lists, and as-built conditions; prepare commissioning reports and turnover packages for operations handover Perform quality assurance reviews of system installations, calibrations, sequences of operations, and safety interlocks Support startup and initial operations of power plants, substations, UPS, generators, or other datacenter critical electrical infrastructure Identify risks and opportunities for improvement in commissioning processes; recommend enhancements to standards, tools, and workflows Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure seamless integration of bespoke AI infrastructure systems (e.g., high-reliability power redundancy, monitoring/EPMS) Maintain a strong focus on safety, compliance with industry codes and standards (NEC, NFPA, etc.), and xAI's operational excellence standards in fast-paced, high-stakes environments BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field (or equivalent practical experience) Proficient in commissioning tools and processes (e.g., test plan development, data logging, fault simulation, and integrated systems testing) Experienced with power systems (medium/low voltage distribution, generators, UPS, switchgear, BESS) Detail-oriented with excellent analytical, problem-solving, and troubleshooting skills under tight deadlines Strong communicator capable of working effectively with multidisciplinary teams, contractors, and stakeholders Comfortable in a dynamic, fast-moving environment with evolving priorities and ambitious timelines PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE: 5+ years of hands-on commissioning experience in mission-critical facilities (data centers, power plants, or similar high-reliability environments), with strong focus on electrical systems Experience commissioning natural gas power generation, datacenter infrastructure, or AI/high-performance compute facilities Familiarity with tools like SEL relays, BMS/EPMS, Navisworks, or commissioning management software Knowledge of redundancy architectures (N+1, 2N), energy efficiency metrics (PUE), and safety protocols in high-voltage environments Previous owner-side or client-facing commissioning role xAI is an equal opportunity employer. ABOUT THE ROLE: As a Fiber Documentation Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, documenting, and optimizing fiber optic networks to support xAI’s high-performance infrastructure. This is a field-intensive role requiring approximately 50% travel. You will conduct site surveys, produce detailed OSP/ISP designs, manage cross-connects, perform basic fiber testing, and ensure accurate records in our tools. You thrive in dynamic environments, pay close attention to detail, and are comfortable working outdoors and in data center or telecom facilities. RESPONSIBILITIES: Document and design fiber networks using IQGeo Network Manager and CAD, including route modeling, data import, and report generation. Conduct site surveys and produce detailed OSP (aerial/underground) and ISP (building entry/riser) connectivity designs in GIS and/or CAD. Survey, document, and install/run jumpers for new and existing fiber cross-connects; maintain accurate records in IQGeo and CAD. Perform basic fiber testing with VFL (fault location) and OPM (power/loss measurement); clean connectors and inspect end-faces. Apply basic OTDR skills and interpret fiber trace reports. Collaborate with teams to install and maintain OXC jumpers in a clean and orderly manner. Collaborate with teams on projects, permitting, and QA; ensure compliance with industry standards. Work with OSP and Construction teams to create, submit, and/or monitor permits. BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: 5+ years of hands-on experience in fiber optic network design, surveying, testing, and OSP/ISP deployments (experience may substitute for formal education). Proficiency with IQGeo Network Manager and CAD or similar geospatial design tools. Experience with fiber testing equipment (VFL, OPM) and cross-connect/jumper installations. Valid driver’s license and physical capability for field work, including walking varied terrain, ladder use, and cable handling. Ability to travel ~50% (including overnights). PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE: Relevant certifications such as CFOT or Fiber Optic Technician. Strong knowledge of fiber optic standards, connector cleaning/inspection, and jumper/cross-connect routing. Experience with NetBox (or similar DCIM/IPAM tools) is a plus. Experience collaborating on permitting processes and QA in telecom or data center environments. Excellent analytical, documentation, and problem-solving skills. Laser safety awareness and commitment to safe work practices. Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, hands-on environment with strong attention to detail. xAI is an equal opportunity employer. ABOUT THE ROLE: The O&M Technician is responsible for the safe, reliable, and efficient operation and maintenance of a 1.2 GW behind-the-meter simple-cycle load following facility consisting of multiple LM6000 aeroderivative gas turbine generators, balance-of-plant systems, and associated electrical distribution. This is a true multi-skilled “hybrid” position requiring proven hands-on expertise in control room operations, mechanical maintenance, electrical, instrumentation & controls (I&C), and water treatment/chemistry for the LM6000 platform. RESPONSIBILITIES: Perform Control Room Operator duties: Safely start, operate, monitor, and shut down LM6000 gas turbines and BOP systems. Execute fast-start dispatches, respond to alarms, and provide effective communication to the facility operations team. Maintain shift logs, LOTO records, and regulatory compliance documentation Perform Mechanical maintenance specific to LM6000: Perform Level 1–2 maintenance, borescope inspections, combustor/TBC inspections, and unit alignment. Replace VBV/IGV actuators, perform lube oil system maintenance, and service starters/clutches Perform Electrical maintenance: Test and maintain 13.8 kV switchgear, generator breakers, and MODs Test exciter, and AVR; calibrate protective relays; support black-start diesel Troubleshoot 480 V MCCs, test motors, and perform infrared thermography Perform Instrumentation & Controls (I&C) duties: Calibrate and troubleshoot vibration, temperature, pressure, and speed sensors Test fire & gas systems (Det-Tronics, etc.) and perform loop checks Perform Water Treatment & Chemistry duties: OperateReverse Osmosis/demineralized water system daily for NOx water, SPRINT, fogging, and water wash system. Test water purity (conductivity, silica, TDS, ph), dose chemicals, and regenerate resin beds Execute LOTO, hot work, confined-space permits Lead or support scheduled LM6000 borescope, combustion, and hot section outages Maintain critical spares inventory and CMMS records. Mentor apprentices and contractors BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: Minimum 2–5 years of direct hands-on experience on LM 6000 aeroderivative turbines (Level 1/2/3 maintenance qualified strongly preferred) Demonstrated competency in at least three of the five core disciplines: Control Room Operations, Mechanical, Electrical, I&C, and Water Treatment/Chemistry Ability to read and interpret LM6000-specific P&IDs, electrical one-line diagrams, Medium-voltage experience is a plus Strong computer skills Valid driver’s license and ability to pass background check, drug screen, respiratory fit test, and physical Physical Requirements: Work at heights, in confined spaces, and around high-voltage equipment Lift 50+ lbs repeatedly, climb turbine enclosures, and wear full PPE including respirator Tolerate high heat and humidity inside LM6000 enclosures and outdoor extreme weather conditions PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE: LM6000 Level 1, 2, or 3 Maintenance Certification DCS Maintenance & Troubleshooting NFPA 70E Electrical Safety Vibration Analyst Category II or higher xAI is an equal opportunity employer. ABOUT THE ROLE: We are seeking a highly skilled Member of Technical Staff to join our team in managing and enhancing reliability across a multi-data center environment. This role focuses on automating processes, building and implementing robust observability solutions, and ensuring seamless operations for mission-critical AI infrastructure. The ideal candidate will combine strong coding abilities with hands-on data center experience to build scalable reliability services, optimize system performance, and minimize downtime—including close partnership with facility operations to address physical infrastructure impacts. If you thrive in lightning-fast, distributed environments and are passionate about leveraging automation to drive efficiency, this is an opportunity to make a significant impact on our infrastructure's resilience and scalability. In an era where AI workloads demand near-zero downtime, this position plays a pivotal role in bridging software engineering principles with physical data center realities. By prioritizing automation and observability, team members in this role can reduce mean time to recovery (MTTR) by up to 50% through proactive monitoring and automated remediation, based on industry benchmarks from high-scale environments like those at hyperscale cloud providers. The primary objective of this team is to mitigate downtime and minimize impact to end-users from both scheduled and unscheduled maintenance, as well as events affecting onsite data centers. This is achieved through proactive automation, robust observability, and integrated software-physical reliability strategies, ensuring our AI infrastructure remains resilient, scalable, and at the cutting edge of innovation. RESPONSIBILITIES: Design, develop, and deploy scalable code and services (primarily in Python and Rust, with flexibility for emerging languages) to automate reliability workflows, including monitoring, alerting, incident response, and infrastructure provisioning. We value adaptability to new tools and paradigms in the fast-evolving AI space. Implement and maintain observability tools and practices, such as metrics collection, logging, tracing, and dashboards, to provide real-time insights into system health across multiple data centers—open to innovative stacks beyond traditional ones like ELK. Collaborate with cross-functional teams—including software development, network engineering, site operations, and facility operations (critical facilities, mechanical/electrical teams, and data center infrastructure management)—to identify reliability bottlenecks, automate solutions for fault tolerance, disaster recovery, capacity planning, and physical/environmental risk mitigation (e.g., power redundancy, cooling efficiency, and environmental monitoring integration).This role encourages broad skill sets from diverse technical backgrounds to foster innovation. Troubleshoot and resolve complex issues in data center environments, including hardware failures, environmental anomalies, software bugs, and network-related problems, while adhering to reliability principles like error budgets and SLAs.** Key Insight: By applying SWE rigor to troubleshooting, team members can create reusable diagnostic tools that accelerate resolution, turning unscheduled events (e.g., hardware faults) into opportunities for system hardening and reducing overall end-user impact through targeted SLAs that prioritize critical AI services. We seek versatile problem-solvers who adapt to bleeding-edge challenges. Optimize Linux-based systems for performance, security, and reliability, including kernel tuning, container orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes or emerging alternatives), and scripting for automation. Understand network topologies and concepts in large-scale, multi-data center environments to effectively troubleshoot connectivity, routing, redundancy, and performance issues; integrate observability into data center interconnects and facility-level controls for rapid diagnosis and automation.** Key Insight: In multi-site setups, network insights allow for automated failover mechanisms that handle both digital and physical disruptions, ensuring seamless continuity for end-users during events like fiber cuts or power outages. This attracts candidates from varied networking and systems backgrounds to drive forward-thinking solutions. Participate in on-call rotations, post-incident reviews (blameless postmortems), and continuous improvement initiatives to enhance overall site reliability, including joint exercises with facility teams for physical failover and recovery scenarios. We prioritize growth-minded individuals who embrace evolving practices. Mentor junior team members and document processes to foster a culture of automation, knowledge sharing, and adaptability to new technologies. BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a closely related technical field (or equivalent professional experience). 5+ years of hands-on experience in site reliability engineering (SRE), infrastructure engineering, DevOps, or systems engineering, preferably supporting large-scale, distributed, or production environments. Strong programming skills with proven production experience in Python (required for automation and tooling); experience with Rust or willingness to work in Rust is a plus, but strong coding fundamentals in at least one systems-level language (e.g., Python, Go, C++) are essential. Solid experience with Linux systems administration, performance tuning, kernel-level understanding, and scripting/automation in production environments. Practical knowledge of containerization and orchestration technologies, such as Docker and Kubernetes (or similar systems). Experience implementing observability solutions, including metrics, logging, tracing, monitoring tools (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, or alternatives), alerting, and dashboards. Familiarity with troubleshooting complex issues in distributed systems, including software bugs, hardware failures, network problems, and environmental factors. Understanding of networking fundamentals (TCP/IP, routing, redundancy, DNS) in large-scale or multi-site environments. Experience participating in on-call rotations, incident response, post-incident reviews (blameless postmortems), and reliability practices such as error budgets or SLAs. Ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams (software engineers, network teams, site/facility operations, mechanical/electrical teams). PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE: 7+ years of experience in SRE or infrastructure roles, ideally in hyperscale, cloud, or AI / ML training infrastructure environments with multi-data center setups. Hands-on experience operating or scaling Kubernetes clusters (or equivalent orchestration) at large scale, including automation for provisioning, lifecycle management, and high-availability. Proficiency in Rust for systems programming and performance-critical components. Direct experience integrating software reliability tools with physical data center infrastructure (e.g., power, cooling, environmental monitoring, facility controls) and automating responses to physical events. Exposure to advanced or innovative observability stacks beyond traditional tools (e.g., exploring cutting-edge alternatives for metrics, logs, and tracing). Experience building automated remediation, fault tolerance, disaster recovery, capacity planning, or predictive failure detection systems. Background in optimizing Linux-based systems for AI workloads, GPU clusters, or high-throughput compute environments. Demonstrated success reducing downtime, MTTR, or improving resource efficiency (e.g., through automation or observability) in high-stakes production settings. Prior work with bare-metal provisioning, data center interconnects, or hybrid/multi-site failover mechanisms. Mentoring experience, strong documentation skills, and a track record of fostering knowledge sharing and automation culture. Comfort with rapid technology adaptation in fast-evolving domains like AI infrastructure. xAI is an equal opportunity employer. For details on data processing, view our Recruitment Privacy Notice .
